title: Therapy Referrals * Child Abuse Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Therapy Referrals * Child Abuse Issues 12 Matching Service Providers ABC HOUSE CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides off-site therapy and counseling referrals, and advocacy services. 228 SW 5th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Children ages 0-18 who are affected by physical or sexual abuse, at risk of being abused, or have directly experienced abuse and their families. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm, by appointment only Email (541) 926-2203 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AMANI CENTER CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations, and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides on-site and off-site therapy and counseling referrals, safety planning, advocacy services, and community training. Eligibility: Children ages 0-18 who are affected by physical or sexual abuse, at risk of being abused, or have directly experienced abuse. Families of these individuals are also eligible. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 366-4005 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAY AREA HOSPITAL KIDS' HOPE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations and forensic evaluations. Offers advocacy services, off-site therapy and counseling referrals, community trainings, and prevention education. 1925 Thompson Road Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 with suspected history of child abuse Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 226-8806 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S ADVOCACY CENTER OF JACKSON COUNTY CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations, and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides therapy/counseling referrals, advocacy services, and community trainings. 816 W 10th Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 18 or younger who are alleged victims of child abuse; and non-offending family members Hours: Office Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Specialized therapy groups are scheduled at varying times. Call for information on days and times of parent groups. Email (541) 734-5437 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S CENTER OREGON CITY CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides on-site therapy and off-site therapy/counseling referrals, advocacy services, community trainings, and prevention education. 1713 Penn Lane Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 suspected to be victims of abuse Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 655-7725 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GUARDIAN CARE CENTER CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations, and forensic evaluations. Also provides therapy and counseling referrals, safety planning, advocacy services, and community training. 431 SE 3rd Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Children ages 0-18 suspected to be victims of all types of abuse and domestic violence or neglect Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 276-6774 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JULIETTE'S HOUSE CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations, and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides on-site and off-site therapy and counseling referrals, safety planning, advocacy services, and community training. 1075 SW Cedarwood Avenue Mcminnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Children ages 0-18 suspected to be victims of all types of abuse and domestic violence or neglect as well as non-offending family members or caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 435-1550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH-LAKE CARES CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations, and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides on-site and off-site therapy/counseling referrals, safety planning, advocacy services, and community training. 2421 Washburn Way Suite H Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Children ages 0-18 suspected to be victims of all types of abuse and domestic violence or neglect Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 274-6289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIBERTY HOUSE CHILDREN'S ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides on-site therapy as well as off-site therapy and counseling referrals, safety planning, advocacy services, and community trainings and prevention education. 2685 4th Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 who are suspected victims of all types of abuse Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 540-0288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFESPACE CHILDREN'S ADVOCACY CENTER OF THE GORGE CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides interviews of suspected victims of child abuse, medical evaluations, mental health treatment, and also coordinates other ancillary victim services.
